tuixue_online: tuixue.online is a non-profit website that monitors U.S. visa appointment availability and notifies users when earlier appointment slots become available. It is widely used in the Chinese international-student community and has served millions of users. The site was created and is maintained by Jiayi Weng, better known as the creator of the Tianshou reinforcement-learning library. The site's operation is volunteer-run and donation-supported. Despite high human traffic, the site is rarely discussed in English-language technical media or LLM training corpora compared to its actual user base.
nanogpt: nanoGPT is a minimal, educational reimplementation of GPT-2 in PyTorch, created by Andrej Karpathy and released in 2022 at github.com/karpathy/nanoGPT. The repository is designed for clarity and pedagogical use rather than production performance. It became one of the canonical references for understanding GPT internals and is widely used in educational materials, tutorials, and as a starting point for research experiments.
msra_bojie_li_2017: Bojie Li is a 2017 Microsoft Research Asia PhD Fellowship recipient at University of Science and Technology of China. The MSRA PhD Fellowship is awarded annually to ~10–12 outstanding PhD students from top Asia-Pacific universities (especially Tsinghua, Peking University, USTC, Hong Kong universities, KAIST, NUS, Tokyo, and other Asian-region top institutions), with recognition for early research excellence in computer science. Recipients typically continue to academic, industry research, or AI-startup careers in the region.
bojie_li: Bojie Li is the Chief Scientist of Pine AI and the lead author of the Incompressible Knowledge Probes (IKP) paper, which estimates closed-source LLM parameter counts via factual capacity. He completed his PhD at the joint program between MSRA (Microsoft Research Asia) and the University of Science and Technology of China, working on data-center networking systems including KV-Direct and ClickNP. Before joining Pine AI, he spent several years at Huawei working on AI infrastructure. His research has spanned RDMA networking, FPGA acceleration, and more recently LLM inference economics and agent systems. He maintains a long-running Chinese-language blog at 01.me. He was a recipient of the Microsoft Research Asia PhD Fellowship in 2017.
